purpose: Per-language parity verdict for the 4 new sink_types (HtmlOutput, FileOpen, HttpRequest, Deserialize). Counts compare vuln.rs L290-L780 source-of-truth tuples against AstSinkPattern entries added in taint.rs.
matrix_source: plan.md §4.2 X-marks matrix; per-language sink lists from vuln.rs L290-L780
method: For each (lang, vuln_type) pair, count vuln.rs's sink-table entries (vuln_rs_count) and the corresponding AstSinkPattern entry-line count (ast_count, where multiple member_patterns/call_names within a single AstSinkPattern are summed across the underlying tuples). Verdict 'parity' = full coverage; 'shape_fit_gap' = vuln.rs entry exists but no AST shape in current grammar can structurally express it; 'subset_no_overlap' = some entries deferred to existing variant (FileWrite) per dispatch-contract M2 line 156 no-double-coverage mandate.

Python_Xss.notes: Markup, mark_safe (call_names); |safe (raw-fallback for Jinja filter syntax)

TypeScript_Xss.carry_forward: innerHTML / document.write / dangerouslySetInnerHTML deliberately NOT re-added as HtmlOutput — they already fire as FileWrite (regex-removal-v1 W1-M1 wired the closest available enum variant before HtmlOutput existed). Per dispatch-contract M2 line 156 (no double-coverage), they are NOT duplicated. M3 vuln_type_from_sink projection or a future reclassification milestone may flip these. M2 adds the 3 non-overlapping shapes: outerHTML, document.writeln, *.html().

TypeScript_Ssrf.notes: fetch, got, axios bare calls; axios.{get,post,put,delete,request}, http.{get,request}, https.{get,request}, superagent.get; node-fetch hyphenated raw-fallback

Rust_PathTraversal.notes: All scoped_identifier paths via raw-fallback (matching established Rust convention): std::fs::read_to_string, std::fs::write, File::open, PathBuf::from

Rust_Ssrf.notes: *.get, *.post wildcards for reqwest/ureq client method shape; reqwest::get, reqwest::Client, ureq::{get,post}, hyper::Client, Url::parse via raw-fallback

Ruby_Xss.notes: raw (call_name bare helper); *.html_safe (wildcard receiver for string method); render html: (raw-fallback keyword-argument idiom)

Ruby_Ssrf.notes: open (Kernel#open call_name); Net::HTTP.{get,post,start}, URI.{open,parse}, RestClient.{get,post}, HTTParty.get (raw-fallback scoped paths)

Swift_PathTraversal.notes: All raw-fallback labelled-argument constructor shapes: String(contentsOfFile:, Data(contentsOf:, FileManager.default.contents(atPath:

Csharp_Xss.carry_forward_advisory: Razor @Html.Raw( is matched via raw-fallback ("", "@Html.Raw(") since the @-prefix is Razor template-syntax that isn't a structural call_expression in tree-sitter-c-sharp. This works for cshtml-embedded usage when the descendant's text spans the @ symbol; if a future grammar refinement separates the @ token, the raw-fallback remains the canonical fit. Documented in M2-carry-forward.json per validator mandate razor_java_constructor_carry_forward_documented.

Php_Xss.carry_forward_advisory: echo / print / <?= matched via raw-fallback at the AST descendant text level. Var-extraction limitation (sink-detection at L3978-L3987 requires a `var` to be extracted; PHP echo_statement / print_intrinsic are not call_kinds and have no structural arguments list) means the sink may not fire on echo $x; until M3 wires shape-aware var extraction. The AST patterns are correctly entered per vuln.rs; a M3 var-extraction extension or M5 carry-forward addresses the firing. Documented in M2-carry-forward.json.

Php_PathTraversal.notes: fopen, file_get_contents, file_put_contents (call_names); include(, require( (raw-fallback language constructs)

Php_Ssrf.notes: fopen, file_get_contents, curl_exec, curl_setopt, get_headers, readfile (call_names — fopen and file_get_contents deliberately appear in BOTH FileOpen and HttpRequest banks per vuln.rs's dual classification: PHP fopen accepts http:// URLs); Guzzle\Client, ->request( raw-fallback

Lua_PathTraversal.notes: io.open (member-access); dofile, loadfile (call_names — also CodeEval per vuln.rs dual file-load/eval classification)

Elixir_Xss.notes: raw bare call_name; Phoenix.HTML.raw structural Module.function via Elixir rfind('.') shape

no_double_coverage_audit.method: For each new AstSinkPattern entry added in M2, manually verified against the existing 6-variant banks (SqlQuery/CodeEval/CodeExec/CodeCompile/ShellExec/FileWrite) for the same language. All conflicts deliberately deferred to FileWrite (TypeScript innerHTML/document.write/dangerouslySetInnerHTML — see TypeScript_Xss verdict).
examples_passing: Python: open( is FileOpen-only (existing FileWrite uses *.write member-shape, no overlap)
PHP: fopen / file_get_contents intentionally fire as BOTH FileOpen AND HttpRequest, mirroring vuln.rs's dual classification (PHP url-fopen wrappers)
